project: Amigos do Trabalho - Desktop Virtual Pet
description: A virtual desktop pet that lives as a transparent overlay, rewarding productivity by converting real keyboard and mouse activity into in-game coins. The pet reacts visually to activity level.

userStories.title: Transparent Always-On-Top Window
userStories.description: As a user, I want the pet to float on my desktop without blocking my work, so that it feels like a companion rather than an obstruction.
acceptanceCriteria: project.godot has display/window/per_pixel_transparency/allowed = true
project.godot has display/window/size/transparent = true
Window is borderless (display/window/size/borderless = true)
Window is always-on-top (display/window/size/always_on_top = true)
Main scene has a root node that sets mouse_passthrough_polygon for transparent regions
Clicking on the pet sprite is captured by Godot (not passed through)
A main scene (main.tscn) is created and set as the project run scene
GDScript files parse without errors
userStories.notes: Use Godot 4.6 project settings for transparency. Use mouse_passthrough_polygon on the Window node for click-through on transparent areas.

userStories.title: GDExtension Build System and Structure
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the GDExtension directory structure, SConstruct build file, and .gdextension configuration so that platform-specific C++ code can be compiled and loaded by Godot.
acceptanceCriteria: Directory structure: gdextension/src/ for C++ source, gdextension/bin/ for compiled output
SConstruct file configured for godot-cpp with platform detection (windows, linux)
global_input.gdextension file in project root registers the extension for both platforms
Header file gdextension/src/register_types.h with initialize/uninitialize declarations
Source file gdextension/src/register_types.cpp that registers GlobalInput singleton
Header file gdextension/src/global_input.h declaring the GlobalInput class with get_key_count(), get_click_count(), reset_counts(), start_hooks(), stop_hooks()
GlobalInput class inherits from Object and uses GDCLASS macro
GDScript files parse without errors
userStories.notes: Use godot-cpp bindings. Platform-specific implementations will be added in US-003 and US-004. Use #ifdef for platform detection. The GlobalInput class should use atomic counters for thread safety.

userStories.title: GDExtension Windows Global Input Hook
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the Windows implementation of GlobalInput that captures system-wide keyboard and mouse events using SetWindowsHookEx.
acceptanceCriteria: gdextension/src/global_input_windows.cpp implements Windows-specific hooks
Uses SetWindowsHookEx with WH_KEYBOARD_LL and WH_MOUSE_LL
Hook runs on a dedicated thread with its own message pump
Key and click counters use atomic integers for thread safety
start_hooks() installs hooks, stop_hooks() uninstalls them cleanly
Code compiles with #ifdef _WIN32 guards
GDScript files parse without errors
userStories.notes: The hook thread needs a message loop (GetMessage/TranslateMessage/DispatchMessage) for low-level hooks to work. Use std::atomic<int> for counters.

userStories.title: GDExtension Linux Global Input Capture
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the Linux implementation of GlobalInput that captures system-wide keyboard and mouse events using X11 XRecord.
acceptanceCriteria: gdextension/src/global_input_linux.cpp implements Linux-specific capture
Uses X11 XRecord extension to capture global key and mouse events
Capture runs on a dedicated thread
Key and click counters use atomic integers for thread safety
start_hooks() begins capture, stop_hooks() stops cleanly
Logs clear error message if XRecord is unavailable or permissions insufficient
Code compiles with #ifdef __linux__ guards
GDScript files parse without errors
userStories.notes: XRecord requires two Display connections. Falls back gracefully with error logging if X11/XRecord not available. Document that input group membership may be needed for /dev/input fallback.

userStories.title: Mock GlobalInput Singleton for Development
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a GDScript mock of GlobalInput so that the game logic can be developed and tested without the compiled C++ extension.
acceptanceCriteria: scripts/mock_global_input.gd implements the same API: get_key_count(), get_click_count(), reset_counts()
Mock generates simulated input counts (e.g., random small numbers each poll)
Autoload registered in project.godot as GlobalInput singleton
Can be easily swapped out for the real GDExtension later by removing the autoload
Includes a debug mode flag to print input counts to console
GDScript files parse without errors
userStories.notes: This mock allows all subsequent GDScript stories to be developed and tested without needing the compiled C++ extension. When the real extension is ready, just remove this autoload.

userStories.title: Coin/Money System
userStories.description: As a user, I want my keystrokes and clicks to earn me coins so that I feel rewarded for being productive.
acceptanceCriteria: scripts/coin_system.gd polls GlobalInput every 1 second using a Timer
Each key press earns configurable coins (default: 1 per keystroke)
Each mouse click earns configurable coins (default: 1 per click)
Coin rates are exported variables for easy configuration
CoinSystem emits a signal when coin total changes
A small Label node near the pet displays the current coin total
CoinSystem is added to the main scene
GDScript files parse without errors
userStories.notes: Use a Timer node with 1.0s wait_time. Poll GlobalInput.get_key_count() and GlobalInput.get_click_count(), then call reset_counts(). Emit coins_changed signal.

userStories.title: Passive Coin Decay
userStories.description: As a user, I want my coins to slowly drain over time during idle periods so that I need to stay active to keep my pet happy.
acceptanceCriteria: Coins decay at configurable rate (default: 1 coin per 30 seconds of zero input)
Decay only occurs when no keystrokes or clicks were registered in the current polling window
Coins cannot go below zero
Decay rate is an exported variable on CoinSystem
Idle time tracking resets when any input is detected
GDScript files parse without errors
userStories.notes: Add idle tracking to the existing CoinSystem. Track seconds since last input. When idle_time >= decay_interval, subtract decay_amount and reset idle timer.

userStories.title: Pet Visual States
userStories.description: As a user, I want the pet to visually react to my activity level so I can tell at a glance how it's doing.
acceptanceCriteria: Pet has at least 3 visual states: Happy (high coins), Neutral (moderate), Sad (low coins)
State thresholds are configurable via exported variables
Pet sprite changes based on current state (sprite swap or modulate color change)
State updates in real time when coins change (connected to coins_changed signal)
scripts/pet.gd manages pet state logic
Placeholder sprites or colored rectangles used for each state
GDScript files parse without errors
userStories.notes: Use simple placeholder visuals (e.g., ColorRect or Sprite2D with modulate). Happy=green tint, Neutral=normal, Sad=blue/gray tint. Connect to CoinSystem.coins_changed signal.

userStories.title: Spend Coins on Pet Interactions
userStories.description: As a user, I want to click the pet and spend coins to feed or play with it, boosting its mood.
acceptanceCriteria: Clicking the pet opens a small popup menu with interaction options (Feed, Play)
Each interaction costs a configurable amount of coins (default: Feed=10, Play=15)
Successful interaction triggers a visual reaction (e.g., scale bounce or color flash)
Interaction temporarily boosts coin balance (adds bonus coins)
Buttons are disabled/grayed when insufficient coins
Menu closes after selecting an interaction or clicking elsewhere
GDScript files parse without errors
userStories.notes: Use a PopupMenu or VBoxContainer that shows/hides on pet click. Connect to CoinSystem to check balance and deduct coins.

userStories.title: Save/Load Pet State
userStories.description: As a user, I want my pet's state and coins to persist when I close and reopen the game.
acceptanceCriteria: On quit, save coin total and pet state to user://save_data.json
On launch, load saved state and restore coin total and pet state
Handle missing save file gracefully (start fresh with defaults)
Handle corrupted/invalid JSON gracefully (start fresh with defaults)
Save triggers on _notification(NOTIFICATION_WM_CLOSE_REQUEST)
GDScript files parse without errors
userStories.notes: Use FileAccess and JSON class. Save to user:// directory. Load on _ready() of a SaveManager autoload or in the main scene.
